The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the San Francisco International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ca/san-francisco/KSFO.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a San Francisco high temperature of 62-63°F at 48.5% implied probability for May 4, aligned with the latest National Weather Service guidance forecasting partly cloudy skies, persistent marine stratus, and west winds of 10-15 mph limiting daytime heating at SFO, the official observing station. Recent observations confirm subdued highs—63°F on May 2 and around 59°F on May 1—under a cool upper-level trough over the California coast, below the typical May average of 64°F. Model ensembles from NOAA's GFS and ECMWF show limited mixing potential, though afternoon clearing could nudge toward 64-65°F (28% odds). Watch for 12z model updates and evening NWS revisions as key catalysts ahead of resolution.

markets.question: Will Nationalist Party win the third-most seats in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Malta on May 30, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the House of Representatives (parliament of Malta) as a result of the 2026 Maltese general election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

Parties will primarily be ranked by the number of seats won. In the event of a tie, the party with the greater number of valid votes will rank higher. If a tie persists, the party whose name appears first in alphabetical order will rank higher. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election, not any coalition it may be a part of.

This market will resolve based on the results of the 2026 Maltese general election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results reported by the Government of Malta, such as those published by the Electoral Commission of Malta (https://electoral.gov.mt/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Prime Minister Robert Abela's snap election call on April 27 for May 30 has intensified focus on Malta's proportional representation contest, with recent polls driving trader consensus that AD+PD will secure third place by national vote share. MaltaToday and Sagalytics surveys show Labour Party at 48-51%, Nationalist Party at 37-46%, AD+PD around 3%, and Momentum at 2%, positioning the green-left alliance ahead of rivals amid their April 17 candidate coordination pact. Aħwa Maltin's recent full-district campaign launch boosted its odds slightly, but low polling keeps it trailing; Imperium Europa and majors remain longshots. Traders weigh narrowing Labour leads and minor party turnout in the tight four-week campaign.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump visit China on May 27, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the calendar date (ET) on which U.S. President Donald Trump next visits China.

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the listed country. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: White House-confirmed schedule positions President Trump's state visit to Beijing for a summit with Xi Jinping on May 14-15, rescheduled from late March due to the US-Iran war, driving trader consensus toward those dates with May 13 at 71.5% implied probability likely reflecting anticipated early arrival logistics after a US C-17 transport landed in Beijing on May 2 for security preparations. Recent US sanctions on Chinese oil refineries prompted China's first invocation of its blocking statute, yet Trump insisted two days ago the trip proceeds "as planned and...amazing" amid bilateral talks and Taiwan warnings from Beijing. Minimal odds on "No visit by May 31" underscore confidence despite escalatory risks, with pre-summit diplomacy prioritizing trade, energy security, and regional stability.

eventMetadata.context_description: Lindsay James commands trader consensus at 93.5% implied probability to win Iowa's 2nd Congressional District Democratic primary on June 2, driven by her dominant fundraising—over $742,000 raised this cycle per recent FEC reports—far outpacing rivals like Kathy Dolter and Clint Twedt-Ball, coupled with fresh endorsements from the Iowa AFL-CIO and Machinists union in late April. As a state House representative from key Dubuque County with established name recognition in this open seat race, she benefits from consolidated party support amid low visibility for challengers. While no public primary polls exist, her internal general election surveys showing competitiveness against Republicans reinforce market positioning. Late-breaking scandals, a rival fundraising surge, or unexpectedly high turnout among challenger backers could still shift odds in the final weeks.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve held the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%–3.75% following its April 28–29, 2026 FOMC meeting, marking the third consecutive pause amid accelerating inflation pressures, with March CPI rising 0.9% month-over-month to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest in nearly two years—and core measures at 2.6%. Solid economic growth and a stable unemployment rate of 4.3% have reinforced trader consensus for prolonged higher-for-longer policy, as reflected in CME FedWatch Tool probabilities exceeding 98% for no change at the June 16–17 meeting and futures implying rates near 3.6% through year-end. Key risks include persistent shelter costs and geopolitical oil disruptions; traders eye May CPI data and the June FOMC for signals on potential late-2026 easing.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Olivia Rodrigo's "Drop Dead" debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 dated May 2, securing her spot among eight unique artists to top the chart in 2026 so far, including surprise frontrunner Ella Langley with seven weeks on "Choosin' Texas" via country crossover appeal. Taylor Swift, Bruno Mars, Harry Styles, Bad Bunny, BTS, and Mariah Carey (holiday holdover) round out the early achievers, fueled by streaming surges, radio play, and sales. Trader consensus reflects fierce competition, with climbers like Justin Bieber's stream-heavy "Daisies" and Olivia Dean's airplay gainer "So Easy" threatening upsets. Upcoming Drake's "Iceman" album and Kendrick Lamar projects loom as catalysts, alongside weekly Friday releases and Tuesday chart reveals through December.

markets.question: Will "The Devil Wears Prada 2" score at least 85 on the Rotten Tomatoes Tomatometer?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the displayed Rotten Tomatoes “All Critics” Tomatometer score for The Devil Wears Prada 2 (2026) is at least equal to the specified number at 10:00 AM ET on May 4, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If, for any reason, the resolution data is unavailable at this market's specified end time, the resolution source will be checked until the relevant data is available. This market will resolve to “No” if no data is available by May 8, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

events.title: "The Devil Wears Prada 2" Rotten Tomatoes score?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the displayed Rotten Tomatoes “All Critics” Tomatometer score for The Devil Wears Prada 2 (2026) is at least equal to the specified number at 10:00 AM ET on May 4, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If, for any reason, the resolution data is unavailable at this market's specified end time, the resolution source will be checked until the relevant data is available. This market will resolve to “No” if no data is available by May 8, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following its May 1 wide theatrical release by 20th Century Studios, The Devil Wears Prada 2 has earned a 77% Tomatometer score on Rotten Tomatoes from 224 critic reviews, surpassing the original 2006 film's 75% and achieving Certified Fresh status in early tallies before slight adjustments. Traders note strong praise for Meryl Streep's iconic Miranda Priestly, Anne Hathaway's matured Andy Sachs, Emily Blunt's scene-stealing Emily, and Stanley Tucci's heartfelt Nigel, alongside timely satire on media consolidation and digital disruption in fashion publishing. Criticisms center on familiar beats lacking the debut's razor-sharp bite and occasional momentum lulls. With an 88% audience score, A- CinemaScore, and robust opening weekend tracking, the score may shift as more reviews pour in over the next few days, a common post-premiere dynamic for review aggregators.

markets.question: Will Russia capture all of Kostyantynivka by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the entirety of Kostyantynivka, Donetsk Oblast, (48.528896° N, 37.702514° E) by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET.

Kostyantynivka will be considered captured if the entirety of the municipality is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by the resolution date, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of the municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities with a darker grey line. If all area within the municipality is shaded in red, however, the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this will still qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Once Russia captures the entirety of the specified area, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/p9N3w6yRVfjx6cuZ8

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces reached the outskirts of Kostiantynivka in Donetsk Oblast over the past 48 hours, using infiltration tactics to attempt a foothold amid intensified assaults, but Ukrainian defenders report repelling these advances while maintaining overall control of the city. This key node in Ukraine's "fortress belt"—linking Druzhkivka, Kramatorsk, and Sloviansk—has withstood nearly a year of Russian infantry and drone attacks, including missed deadlines like April 25. Recent Ukrainian counter-maneuvers in the sector, per ISW assessments, have reclaimed some ground, underscoring the dynamic frontline. Traders weigh ongoing escalation risks in the Pokrovsk-Kostiantynivka axis against Ukraine's fortified defenses and resupply challenges, with no major diplomatic breakthroughs altering momentum.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Democratic victory at 92.5¢ in Pennsylvania's 6th Congressional District House race, reflecting incumbent Rep. Chrissy Houlahan's commanding position in a Solid Democratic seat with Cook PVI D+6. Houlahan, an Air Force veteran seeking a fifth term, secured 56% in 2024 and larger margins previously against underfunded challengers, buoyed by Chester County's suburban, college-educated voters who have trended reliably blue. With March filing deadlines passed, her May 19 primary faces no serious opposition, while Republican Marty Young enters without national buzz or fundraising edge. Odds hold firm absent a Houlahan scandal, primary upset, or strong national Republican midterm wave tilting battleground Pennsylvania.

markets.question: Another US debt downgrade before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States' long-term sovereign credit letter rating is downgraded by any of the three major credit rating agencies (S&P, Moody's, Fitch) at any point by December 31, 2026 11:59pm ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Standard & Poor's, Moody's, or Fitch, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 75.5% implied probability against another U.S. sovereign debt downgrade before 2027, reflecting stable outlooks from major agencies—S&P at AA+ stable, Fitch at AA+ stable, and Moody's at Aa1 stable—following Moody's May 2025 cut from Aaa amid fiscal deterioration. Despite Fitch's April 30, 2026, warning of deficits averaging 7.9% of GDP through 2028 and gross debt projected to hit 122% of GDP by end-2027, with public debt now exceeding GDP for the first time since World War II, no further rating actions have materialized, buoyed by the dollar's reserve currency status and institutional inertia. Key swing factors include debt ceiling negotiations and midterm budget battles later in 2026, which could test governance but have yet to shift agency views.

Recent catalysts include the April 4 filing deadline solidifying a competitive GOP primary between state-endorsed Assemblyman Robert Smullen—who self-loaned $500,000 per Q1 reports—and Trump-backed businessman Anthony Constantino, both bolstering party resources; Democrats face a fragmented five-way primary lacking a frontrunner. June 23 primaries loom as key tests for nominee strength in this North Country battleground, where local issues like border trade and farming could sway turnout before the November 3 general election.

markets.question: NK Istra 1961 vs. NK Slaven Belupo: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Prva Liga game between NK Istra 1961 and NK Slaven Belupo, scheduled for May 4 at 12:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if NK Istra 1961 and NK Slaven Belupo combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on hnl.hr. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: NK Istra 1961 vs. NK Slaven Belupo - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Prva Liga game, scheduled for May 4 at 12:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: NK Istra 1961 hosts NK Slaven Belupo in a mid-table HNL clash at Stadion Aldo Drosina, with Slaven holding 5th place and Istra 7th after around 30 matches, both chasing European spots or consolidation amid a tight standings battle behind leaders Dinamo Zagreb and Hajduk Split. Istra enters off a 2-1 home win over Vukovar on April 18 and a tough Dinamo Zagreb fixture on April 22, showing decent home form, while Slaven drew 2-2 at Hajduk Split on April 17 before facing Lokomotiva on April 21, reflecting solid away resilience. Head-to-head records are balanced with frequent draws; Istra's Israel Isaac Ayuma remains sidelined by injury, but no other major absences reported in the past 30 days. Late-season motivation and home advantage could sway this evenly matched encounter.

markets.question: Levante UD vs. CA Osasuna: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga game between Levante UD and CA Osasuna, scheduled for May 8 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Levante UD and CA Osasuna combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Levante UD vs. CA Osasuna - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga game, scheduled for May 8 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Levante UD hosts CA Osasuna in La Liga at Estadi Ciutat de Valencia on May 8, with trader consensus reflecting a tight matchup amid late-season stakes. Levante, battling in the lower table after a 1-0 home win over Getafe on April 13 but a 2-0 loss at Real Sociedad earlier in the month, gains momentum from solid home form while missing winger Roger Brugué to a lingering ligament tear and others like Morales with discomfort. Osasuna, mid-table around 10th with 39 points from 32 games, slumped to a 1-0 defeat at Athletic Club on April 21, compounded by forward Ante Budimir's muscle injury and Iker Benito's ACL absence, though their stronger head-to-head record (8 wins to Levante's 4 in last 15) tempers concerns. Home advantage and Osasuna's travel could sway probabilities ahead of lineups.

markets.question: Sporting CP vs. Vitória SC: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming Primeira Liga game between Sporting CP and Vitória SC, scheduled for May 4 at 3:15 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Sporting CP and Vitória SC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligaportugal.pt. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Sporting CP vs. Vitória SC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Primeira Liga game, scheduled for May 4 at 3:15 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sporting CP, third in the Primeira Liga with 71 points from 29 matches and a league-leading +55 goal difference, host seventh-placed Vitória Guimarães (39 points from 30 games) at Estádio José Alvalade in a pivotal late-season fixture. Traders factor in Sporting's overwhelming head-to-head dominance—36 wins to Vitória's 7—bolstered by home advantage and title-chasing momentum, tempered by a recent 1-2 derby defeat to Benfica on April 19 and Champions League exit (0-1 aggregate vs. Arsenal). Vitória enter with solid recent form, including a 1-0 away victory at Gil Vicente last weekend and a 1-1 draw at AVS, though injuries to Nuno Santos and Iván Fresneda sideline key Sporting depth while Vitória manages A. Ndoye concerns. Schedule spots heighten stakes for European qualification.

markets.question: Elon Bull Run Parlay
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if all of the following conditions are met between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET:

- Elon Musk trillionaire
- Another Elon baby
- 9+ SpaceX Starship launches reach space

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will remain open until it is confirmed that at least one of the above conditions has not been met, or until all of the above conditions have been met within the specified timeframe.

The full rules for this market can be found here: 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Elon+Bull+Run+Parlay.pdf

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors "No" at 86% implied probability for the Elon Bull Run Parlay, reflecting the steep hurdles for all three 2026 conditions: Elon Musk hitting $1 trillion net worth per Bloomberg Billionaires Index, announcing another child, and SpaceX achieving 9+ Starship launches to 62-mile altitude. A recent $35 billion drop in Musk's fortune to around $800 billion—tied to Tesla stock volatility amid delayed Full Self-Driving regulatory approvals and xAI monetization setbacks—has widened the gap to trillionaire status, requiring a massive valuation surge across Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI. No public baby confirmation has emerged this year, while Starship lags with Flight 12 targeting late May after prior delays, jeopardizing the launch cadence. Key catalysts include upcoming Starship tests and potential SpaceX IPO filings, though historical slippage tempers optimism.

markets.question: Will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit (HIGH) $304 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Amazon.com (AMZN) shares hover near $268 after Q1 2026 earnings on April 29 showed 17% revenue growth to $181.5 billion, record 13.1% operating margin, and AWS sales up 28%—beating estimates—yet dipped on plans for $200 billion in 2026 capital expenditures to fuel AI and cloud expansion. This trader consensus reflects optimism in AWS's high-margin trajectory and advertising gains offsetting e-commerce pressures, with analyst average price targets at $310 implying 16% upside potential by year-end. Near-term catalysts include the May 20 annual shareholder meeting and consumer spending trends tied to Federal Reserve policy; watch capex returns and AI chip rollouts for May month-end price dynamics.

markets.question: Will HOTU qualify to IEM Cologne Major 2026?
markets.description:
The IEM Cologne Major 2026 is scheduled to take place from June 2 to June 21, 2026.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for IEM Cologne Major 2026.If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to IEM Cologne Major 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official ESL / Valve rules.

If IEM Cologne Major 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of IEM Cologne Major 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ESL website (https://pro.eslgaming.com/tour/cs/cologne/). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/counterstrike/Intel_Extreme_Masters/2026/Cologne) may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: All 32 teams for IEM Cologne Major 2026 secured direct invites via Valve Regional Standings as of the April 6 snapshot, following final Tier 2 LANs like HLC Belgrade and Urban Riga Open, with no changes in the past 30 days. Stage 3 Legends features top squads Vitality, Natus Vincere, PARIVISION, Falcons, Aurora, MOUZ, FURIA, and The MongolZ; Stage 2 Challengers includes Team Spirit, G2 Esports, Astralis, FUT Esports, Monte, 9z, paiN Gaming, and Legacy; while Stage 1 Contenders packs GamerLegion, BIG, BetBoom, HEROIC, SINNERS, and others like Gaimin Gladiators and THUNDER dOWNUNDER. Notably, FaZe Clan missed out for the first time in a decade. Teams now focus on roster prep ahead of the June 2 Swiss stages and Lanxess Arena playoffs.

markets.question: Will Opendoor (OPEN) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Opendoor is estimated to release earnings on May 7, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Opendoor's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $-0.10 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Opendoor reports GAAP EPS greater than $-0.10 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Opendoor releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 58% implied probability that Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) will miss Q1 2026 earnings consensus of -$0.06 EPS and $667 million revenue, set for release May 7, amid ongoing U.S. housing market challenges like elevated mortgage rates curbing transaction volumes. Despite a Q4 2025 adjusted EPS beat (-$0.07 vs. -$0.09 expected) and recent pending home sales uptick signaling modest demand recovery, traders remain cautious over year-over-year revenue contraction (est. -42%) and persistent margin pressures in the iBuying model. Analyst price targets average $4.81 below current ~$5.50 share price, underscoring limited upside expectations ahead of the report.

markets.question: Jerome Powell out as Fed Chair by May 15, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Jerome Powell ceases to be Chair of the Federal Reserve for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Jerome Powell's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Jerome Powell and the Federal Reserve; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Jerome Powell out as Fed Chair by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Jerome Powell ceases to be the Chair of the U.S. Federal Reserve for any period of time between this market's creation and the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the U.S. Government; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jerome Powell's term as Federal Reserve Chair expires May 15, 2026, after which President Trump-nominated successor Kevin Warsh awaits Senate confirmation to assume the role, reflecting tensions over Fed independence amid Trump's public pressure for rate cuts and personnel changes. In a surprise April 29 announcement during his final Federal Open Market Committee press conference, Powell stated he will remain on the Fed Board of Governors until January 2028, citing legal protections and an ongoing DOJ probe into Fed renovations that bars his resignation. Trump allies, including nominee Scott Bessent, criticized this as an attempt to act as "shadow chair," while a U.S. attorney left open further investigations. Traders weigh the fixed term end against potential post-Chair removal challenges and Warsh's confirmation timeline.
